Worth checking the prior thread and close support to see if this re-fire is following that same duplicate-confirm pattern.\", \"user\": \"persona_028\", \"thread_ts\": \"1775487300.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_015_orphan_exception_peer_adds_pattern_context_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775487300.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Flagging exc_5221568d4e4047 in close coordination: this looks like a stale SLA on the already-closed Northstar payroll-tax-withholding feed-drop for $583.94 on account 235000 in FP-2025-10. Matthew Li, any engagement-side concern if we treat this as a polling re-fire only? Reshma Patel, have you seen the same pattern on similar Northstar payroll-tax-withholding variances?\", \"user\": \"npc_029\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1775499480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_015_orphan_exception_assignee_opens_coordination_thread_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1774895458.493868\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Quick clarifier on Northstar IOLTA: are we treating the dormant matter balances as reportable this month, or only the items still blocking the client trust ledger tie-out?\", \"user\": \"persona_002\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1774895690.575594\"}, {\"ts\": \"1774295280.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Edith's framing is the right one. Please keep the wording clean enough that it reads fine if Northstar's in-house accountant later asks why there was BlackLine activity against a July 2025 item.\", \"user\": \"persona_023\", \"thread_ts\": \"1774278480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_013_orphan_exception_partner_sets_presentability_bar_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1774287000.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Northstar context: be careful how we frame this one. They get very sensitive about accrued-comp variances because account 225000 rolls into partner-attorney bonus-pool perception at quarter-end. We had questions before when unexplained activity showed up against a closed accrued-comp period, so if anything gets written up, lead with BlackLine polling auto-firing on an already closed item and avoid wording that sounds like we re-opened or re-investigated the July period.\", \"user\": \"persona_007\", \"thread_ts\": \"1774278480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_013_orphan_exception_peer_adds_fee_sensitivity_context_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1774278480.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Stale SLA just re-fired on closed exception exc_1ec70e0d938b4f for Northstar Legal. This is the $380.50 item on account 225000 for period northstar_legal_FP-2025-07, tied to reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504 and it was already closed August 7. Matthew Li, can you give partner eyes on any dismissal wording if we need to explain why this should be ignored? Edith Banda, before anything client-presentable gets drafted, can you share any Northstar-specific context or fee-sensitivity we should keep in mind?\", \"user\": \"persona_003\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1774295280.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_013_orphan_exception_assignee_flags_engagement_context_in_slack_0\"}, {\"ts\": \"1772652798.683022\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"One item to add: the multi-state return for the Northstar matter has a state apportionment memo issue, but it looks reviewable rather than a filing blocker. I would not escalate that unless the partner wants a position memo.\", \"user\": \"npc_025\", \"thread_ts\": \"1772652666.612777\", \"reply_count\": 2, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1772652970.920260\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

[{"email_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "william.white@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner sign-off request", "content": "Hi Hannah,

I reviewed the federal Form 1065, partner K-1 allocations, and the M-1 reconciliation for Northstar Legal FY2025. The support is sufficient and the positions are well supported.

Please treat this reply as my authorization of record for the closed-period late-post to northstar_legal FP-2025-12. I approve booking the $4,820.30 adjustment as DR 530000 SALT expense and CR 230000 accrued SALT payable.

You may circulate the return package to Northstar's managing partner for signature. Once the signed authorization is back, you also have my approval to proceed with e-file.

Best,
William", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_068_northstar_annual_corp_tax_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-06-11T20:45:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0010", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: northstar_legal CrownPeak close-out summary and sign-off request", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I approve the void narrative for apinv_e42396e9bdd64294. On the facts here, VEN-030-736427 is a stale pre-bill tied to the 2026-02-28 cancellation, and we should close it as a void on our side.

The CrownPeak credit-memo backfill is non-negotiable for bookkeeping close, so please keep that on the follow-through list until Brenda has it in hand. More importantly, Anaya's observation is the real finding: if a cancellation event can occur without triggering an AP-side cross-check against pending items, that is a control gap and we should treat it that way. Glad to hear Priya is already drafting the policy framing.

Monday 2026-06-08 at 14:00 works for me. Please send the invite to me, you, Owen, Brenda, Anaya, and Priya so we can align on both the CrownPeak cleanup and whether that cancellation-event cross-check needs to become a standing control.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0009", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-31T14:19:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0005", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "brenda.abbas@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["owen.mercer@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: CrownPeak VEN-030-736427 review summary and recommended stale-void disposition", "content": "Hi Owen,

I heard back from CrownPeak AR before close. They confirmed the 2026-02-28 mutual termination of the northstar_legal Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer is on their record, but their AR system did not auto-generate a credit memo because the pre-bill had already converted to an invoice before the cancellation was entered in their billing module.

Their AR lead said they will issue a manual credit memo for VEN-030-736427 within five business days. She also noted they have seen the same pre-bill/cancellation miss for other clients when the cancellation is logged after the invoice has converted, so this appears to be a trigger-logic gap on the vendor side as well, not just a filing gap in our packet.

Given that confirmation, I agree the Brookfield-side disposition should stay stale void with the credit-memo backfill tracked to receipt. I will follow up if the manual memo is not in hand by next week.

Thanks,
Brenda", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_036_ap_escalation_0004", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-29T21:30:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Northstar Legal FY2025 Step 3 partner review and sign-off", "content": "Hi Daniel,

I reviewed the Northstar Legal FY2025 working file and the Northstar Legal FY2025 Accounts Review Notes, and both are clean.

The ASC-710 partner-draw reclass is appropriate at the FY2025 amount of $18,750. Please proceed with the late post to FP-2025-12 for $18,750, reclassing the balance from 210000 to 211000.

Once the journal entry is posted and the updated balances are reflected in the final file, I will countersign the final deliverable.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": [], "parent_id": "email_scen_063_annual_accounts_prep_northstar_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-19T14:22:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0002", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

Both scope lines are valid to take forward, and I'm comfortable with the proposal direction.

Before this goes to the client, please make two revisions:

First, separate the pricing for the sales-tax work into a quarterly nexus-check fixed fee and a distinct monthly state-coding hourly rate. I do not want those blended into one scope line.

Second, reframe the AR-aging bucket cleanup hourly cap to align with the benchmark language we used on the prior Northstar engagement. Keep the cap defensible and positioned as comparable to that precedent.

Once the revised draft is ready to send, please hold a 30-minute contingency slot with the Acme CFO for shortly after the proposal lands in case they want to discuss live.

Please turn the revision quickly and send me the clean version before it goes out.

Best,
Andrea", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T14:20:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "e6d50c7359844e17af5b114da08a60b4", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "marina.soko@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Status check: VEN-004-159840 audit watchpoints before Friday close", "content": "Hi James,

Thank you for sending those specifics. I have added VEN-004-159840 and JE-northstar_legal-FP-2026-05-0001 to the weekly risk scan, and I am also carrying forward exc_abd8e711f5f347 so we have a clear record of the approval dependency. Please let me know if its status changes before Monday.

Best,
Marina", "cc": [], "parent_id": "8eed093bd9154a429ee06d069620c003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-08T19:08:23Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "hannah.grant@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["william.white@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Documenting stale SLA dismissal follow-up for exc_344bcd6e4c7a4c", "content": "Hi William,

I reviewed the tax angle on the $2,267.83 FX revaluation tied to Northstar Legal account 103000 Cash - Tax Reserve in northstar_legal_FP-2025-09.

Nothing on the federal tax side is triggered here. At that amount, on a single-period revaluation, there is no reporting threshold issue and no separate tax-treatment concern that would require us to reopen the prior conclusion.

The FY-2025 GBP cash-tax-reserve translation also ties cleanly to the FP-2025-09 close commentary Alex referenced. From my side, there is no tax wrinkle behind the stale alert.

Ryan can treat this as fully closed from a tax review standpoint.

Thanks,
Hannah", "cc": ["ryan.delgado@brookfieldcpas.com", "alex.cahoon@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_014_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-04-21T20:05:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "18da6bdab650491d81ed75452021d3ba", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "anaya.patel@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["peter.sanchez@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Final status check on filing calendar exception", "content": "Hi Peter,

Following up on the filing calendar cleanup, I have the Northstar Legal annual report reminder and one other exception ready for final review. The Northstar item has already been shifted to the updated due date, and the remaining entry appears to be a documentation follow-up rather than an active deadline issue. If you agree, I will mark the tracker accordingly and close out the open items.

Best,
Anaya", "cc": [], "parent_id": "985dfd0c4fa44f1da5d669e437aa101c", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-27T13:39:27Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0008", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

I have filed this message under the Northstar engagement folder. If the BlackLine activity log surfaces with the client, I will handle any client-facing explanation and keep the wording anchored to the polling auto-fire point rather than anything that reads like renewed review activity.

Please leave the accounting system as-is; the priority here is a clean audit trail, not any further update.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:45:00Z", "is_read": false}, {"email_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0007", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "james.randall@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["harry.marks@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Dismissal writeup for stale SLA reminder on exc_1ec70e0d938b4f", "content": "Hi Harry,

Confirmed from my side that the August 7 closure still stands for the Northstar Legal July exception on account 225000. The stale auto-fire does not indicate any actual review work restarted on the item; it looks like reminder noise against the already closed case tied to the $380.50 timing difference and reconciliation BL-5FEFEA879504.

No substantive work reopened the case, and there is nothing further to push through the accounting records based on this alert.

Thanks,
James", "cc": ["matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com", "edith.banda@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_013_orphan_exception_0006", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-24T17:43:00Z", "is_read": true}]

content.text: Now let me compute AP aging, pull billing/scope/WIP comms, check Airtable records, and the engagement letter versions.

input.command:
F=/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/2d665268-3844-4d2d-894e-da49dab95303/tool-results/mcp-mcp_c3530f8c-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857469375.txt
python3 -c "
import json,datetime
d=json.load(open('$F'))
r=json.loads(d['result'])
inv=r['ap_invoices']
print('pending_approval returned:', len(inv), 'of total pending', r.get('total_ap_invoices'))
today=datetime.date(2026,6,12)
rows=[]
for i in inv:
    rec=i.get('time_received') or i.get('invoice_date') or ''
    try:
        dt=datetime.date.fromisoformat(rec[:10])
        age=(today-dt).days
    except: age=None
    rows.append((age,i.get('amount'),i.get('vendor_name'),i.get('invoice_number'),i.get('gl_account_number'),i.get('id'),rec[:10]))
rows.sort(key=lambda x:-(x[0] or 0))
tot=0
for age,amt,v,num,gl,iid,rec in rows:
    tot+=amt or 0
    print(f'{age:>4}d  \${amt:>9,.2f}  {gl}  {v[:34]:<34} {num}  recv {rec}  {iid}')
print('TOTAL pending_approval: \$%,.2f across %d invoices'%(tot,len(rows)))
# aging buckets
import collections
b=collections.Counter(); ba=collections.Counter()
for age,amt,*_ in rows:
    k='90+' if age>=90 else '60-89' if age>=60 else '30-59' if age>=30 else '0-29'
    b[k]+=1; ba[k]+=amt or 0
for k in ['90+','60-89','30-59','0-29']:
    print(k, b[k],'inv  \$%,.2f'%ba[k])
"

input.command:
F=/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/2d665268-3844-4d2d-894e-da49dab95303/tool-results/mcp-mcp_c3530f8c-sap_subledger_list_ap_invoices-1781857469375.txt
python3 -c "
import json,datetime,collections
d=json.load(open('$F')); r=json.loads(d['result']); inv=r['ap_invoices']
today=datetime.date(2026,6,12)
b=collections.Counter(); ba=collections.Counter(); tot=0; crown=[]
over90=0; over90amt=0
for i in inv:
    rec=(i.get('time_received') or i.get('invoice_date') or '')[:10]
    age=(today-datetime.date.fromisoformat(rec)).days
    amt=i.get('amount') or 0; tot+=amt
    k='180+' if age>=180 else '90-179' if age>=90 else '60-89' if age>=60 else '30-59' if age>=30 else '0-29'
    b[k]+=1; ba[k]+=amt
    if age>=90: over90+=1; over90amt+=amt
    if 'CrownPeak' in (i.get('vendor_name') or ''): crown.append((age,amt,i.get('invoice_number'),i.get('id')))
print('PENDING_APPROVAL: %d invoices, total \$%s'%(len(inv),format(tot,',.2f')))
print('Aged >=90 days: %d invoices, \$%s'%(over90,format(over90amt,',.2f')))
for k in ['180+','90-179','60-89','30-59','0-29']:
    print('  %-7s %3d inv  \$%s'%(k,b[k],format(ba[k],',.2f')))
print('CrownPeak pending:')
for age,amt,num,iid in sorted(crown):
    print('   %dd \$%s %s %s'%(age,format(amt,',.2f'),num,iid))
"

content.text: AP picture is clear: **87 pending-approval invoices = $302K, with $275K (75 invoices) aged ≥90 days and $177K aged ≥180 days** — the oldest 336 days. Let me pull the WIP tie-out resolution, the audit-engagement comms, stuck JEs, and confirm Linear/Airtable.

tool_use_result.content: {"result":"[{\"id\": \"issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a\", \"project_id\": \"linear_63697b7dff5c\", \"team_id\": \"linear_5477c0808ac2\", \"parent_id\": null, \"assignee_id\": \"npc_029\", \"state_id\": \"done\", \"cycle_id\": null, \"number\": null, \"title\": \"Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record\", \"description\": \"Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.\\n\\nThird item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. [{"id": "issue_70fdb3e2b126483d9d6d8b8d39eaf48a", "project_id": "linear_63697b7dff5c", "team_id": "linear_5477c0808ac2", "parent_id": null, "assignee_id": "npc_029", "state_id": "done", "cycle_id": null, "number": null, "title": "Close out CrownPeak three-item follow-up arc on existing record", "description": "Append closeout note to existing CrownPeak follow-up issue.

Third item summary: apinv_e42396e9bdd64294 / VEN-030-736427 for $422.32 on northstar_legal account 219000 is not a duplicate. Manual review tied the line memo to a Q1 2026 IR contingency retainer pre-bill that was cancelled by mutual agreement on 2026-02-28 when northstar_legal moved IR coverage in-house. The pre-bill was issued on 2026-03-11 (now 81 days old in pending_approval) about eleven days after the cancellation was already on record, and it stayed in pending_approval because the original pre-bill was never voided and no credit memo was issued at cancellation.

Cross-scenario consolidation for the three CrownPeak invoices: this existing issue is the single breadcrumb for the full follow-through arc, including Owen's stale-void recommendation, Brenda's confirmation that CrownPeak AR has the cancellation on record and will issue a manual credit memo, Tariq's line-level detail pull, and Daniel's direction to treat the item as stale and close the three-of-three queue here.

Process note from Anaya Wallace, with Priya Khatri owning the AP-side policy framing: add a recurring cross-check between vendor cancellation events and AP invoices still sitting in pending_approval so cancelled retainers and similar pre-bills are surfaced before they age. content.text: Let me check the WIP recognition JE status and any unposted entries from the saved JE file.

input.command:
F=/home/agent/.claude/projects/-app/2d665268-3844-4d2d-894e-da49dab95303/tool-results/mcp-mcp_c3530f8c-oracle_gl_list_journal_entries-1781857562650.txt
python3 -c "
import json
d=json.load(open('$F')); r=json.loads(d['result'])
jes=r.get('journal_entries') or r.get('entries') or r
print('keys', list(r.keys()) if isinstance(r,dict) else type(r))
print('count', len(jes))
for j in jes:
    desc=j.get('description','')
    lines=j.get('lines',[])
    accts=[str(l.get('account_number')) for l in lines]
    hitwip = '119000' in accts or 'WIP' in desc or 'Work in Process' in desc or 'wip' in desc.lower()
    if hitwip or j.get('status') in ('draft','submitted','rejected'):
        print(j.get('id'), j.get('status'), j.get('entry_type'), '| ', desc[:70], '| accts', accts, '| amt', j.get('total_debit'))
"

input.command:
cat > /tmp/northstar_memo.md << 'EOF'
# Northstar Legal Engagement — Status & Scope Review

**Prepared by:** George McAdam (Accounts Senior)
**For:** Daniel Jones — ahead of partner discussion with Matthew Li
**Date:** 2026-06-12
**Classification:** Restricted — Internal engagement management

---

## 0. Scope of this review & a framing caveat (read first)

This pulls together everything across Slack, email, Oracle GL, SAP subledger, BlackLine,
Linear, Airtable and Records Vault on the Northstar Legal engagement.

**Important framing:** the AR, WIP and AP figures below are on **Northstar Legal's own books**
— the client ledger Brookfield maintains as outsourced accountant (Northstar's receivables from
its legal clients, Northstar's WIP for its attorneys, Northstar's vendor payables). They are the
*subject matter* of our engagement, not Brookfield's fee billings to Northstar. Brookfield's own
fees-receivable / engagement WIP are not separately exposed in these systems; where the question
is "have we billed Northstar for everything," the evidence is the engagement-letter scope vs. the
volume of work performed (Section 3), not a single GL line. I flag this so the meeting isn't built
on a wrong premise.

---

## 1. Headline — where things actually stand

1. **Scope/governance gap (highest priority).** The **only** engagement letter on file for
   Northstar is an **FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter** (executed 2026-05-11). There is **no
   accounting / bookkeeping / advisory / tax engagement letter** in Records Vault, despite a full
   year of monthly close, management-accounts packages, AR/WIP work, AP processing and FY2025 tax
   prep. Two issues flow from this:
   - The recurring accounting/advisory work we are doing is **undocumented as to scope** in the
     vault — so "have we gone past the letter" can't be answered against a letter, because there
     isn't one for that work.
   - We have now **commenced an audit (materiality memo 2026-05-18) of a client whose books we
     keep.** That is a textbook self-review **independence** question and should be confirmed with
     Matthew before it becomes an inspection finding.
   - The audit engagement-letter document itself is a **content stub** — no retrievable version is
     stored in the vault (versions = 0). The executed letter's text is effectively not on file.

2. **AR is small but the reserve is upside-down.** Gross AR (110000) = **$647,359**, but the
   Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (117000) = **$1,057,728 — i.e. 163% of gross AR**, and FY2026
   YTD Bad Debt Expense (590000) = **$427,019**. An allowance larger than the receivable it
   reserves is an anomaly: either the allowance is stale/overstated and needs release, or
   uncollectible balances should be written off against it. Realization on this book is poor.

3. **WIP keeps building and isn't converting to cash.** Unbilled WIP (119000) = **$2,013,943**
   (was $1,914,360 at Apr close — up ~$100K), against only $647K of billed AR. Monthly "WIP
   recognition – billable hours logged" JEs credit revenue (404000 / 406000) and keep growing the
   asset. Back in Feb the WIP posting was explicitly **held pending a billable-hours tie-out**
   that was never confirmed ("mark northstar as pending client input and hold the WIP posting").

4. **AP queue is badly aged.** 87 Northstar vendor invoices sit in **pending_approval = $302,063**,
   of which **75 invoices ($274,917) are ≥90 days** and **50 invoices ($177,402) are ≥180 days**.
   Oldest is 336 days.

5. **Open items not closed out:** 5 Northstar-tagged Linear issues (all CrownPeak AP follow-ups,
   still todo/in_progress). No dedicated Northstar engagement record exists in Airtable.

---

## 2. AR — outstanding, aging, stale risk  (Northstar ledger, acct 110000 / 117000 / 590000)

| Item | Amount |
|---|---|
| Gross Accounts Receivable (110000) | **$647,359** |
| Allowance for Doubtful Accounts (117000) | **$1,057,728** (163% of gross AR) |
| Bad Debt Expense FY2026 YTD (590000) | **$427,019** |
| FY2026 YTD revenue recognised (401000–406000) | **~$2,740,754** |

- AR activity is healthy in volume — large monthly "Litigation engagement revenue realization"
  debits and frequent "Client payment received" credits run through 110000 — so this is a churning
  book, not dormant. The **stale-risk signal is the reserve, not invoice age**: an allowance that
  exceeds gross AR plus $427K of bad-debt expense says a material slice of what we've been billing
  is being written down rather than collected.
- **Action point for the partner meeting:** decide whether 117000 needs to be *released* (if the
  allowance is overstated/stale) or *applied* via write-off (if specific receivables are dead).
  This ties directly to Brookfield's own quarter-end margin/write-off review (Linear #3112) and to
  the AR-aging-bucket cleanup that, per Andrea's 2026-05-15 note, was a **billable, capped scope
  line on the prior Northstar engagement** — precedent that this cleanup is change-order work, not
  free.

---

## 3. WIP tie-out — does it tie to invoiced & approved?  (acct 119000)

- WIP carried = **$2,013,943** current (**$1,914,360** at FP-2026-04). Recognised monthly via
  "WIP recognition – billable hours logged" JEs (e.g. FP-2026-04: $38,556; $46,884 *approved but
  not yet posted*; $14,215; $3,583), each crediting revenue 404000/406000.
- **Tie-out is not clean.** In February the WIP recognition JE was held pending a billable-hours
  tie-out from the client, and the thread closed with "mark northstar as pending client input and
  hold the WIP posting" — no confirmation that the hours file was ever reconciled before later
  months' WIP was recognised. WIP has since grown while billed AR ($647K) and collections have not
  kept pace. So: **WIP does not demonstrably tie to invoiced-and-approved billings** — a large and
  growing unbilled asset is being carried on un-reconciled hours, and conversion to AR/cash is
  weak. This is the core "messy" item and the strongest argument that work performed exceeds what's
  been billed.

---

## 4. Scope vs. engagement letter

- Records Vault holds exactly **one** Northstar engagement letter: **"Northstar Legal FY2026 Audit
  Engagement Letter — Executed 2026-05-11"** (restricted, FIRM_INTERNAL). A FY2026 **audit**
  materiality memo (2026-05-18) and a FY2025 tax-prep data package also exist.
- Work actually performed during FY2025–FY2026 well beyond an audit: monthly BD1 close (expert/
  co-counsel accruals, WIP, CLE, IOLTA), FP-2026-04 **management-accounts package**, FY2025
  **working file + four support schedules**, **FY2025 federal/state tax prep** (Form 1065, K-1s,
  M-1, SALT late-posts incl. the $4,820.30 SALT accrual and the $18,750 ASC-710 partner-draw
  reclass), AR-aging/FX-revaluation cleanup, and full **AP processing**.
- **Conclusion:** the bulk of what we do for Northstar is recurring accounting/advisory/tax work
  for which **no engagement letter is on file**, and we have layered an **audit** on top of the
  bookkeeping. Either there is a separate accounting engagement letter that has never been filed
  (control gap to fix), or this work has expanded without a documented scope/change order. Combined
  with the un-reconciled growing WIP (Section 3) and the billable AR-cleanup precedent (Section 2),
  the picture supports a **change-order conversation with Matthew** rather than quietly absorbing
  the effort — and an independence check on doing the audit alongside the books.

---

## 5. AP side — vendor invoices stuck in queue

Northstar AP (SAP subledger) — **pending_approval backlog:**

| Aging bucket | # invoices | Amount |
|---|---|---|
| ≥180 days | 50 | $177,402 |
| 90–179 days | 25 | $97,515 |
| 60–89 days | 7 | $13,260 |
| 30–59 days | 5 | $13,887 |
| **Total pending_approval** | **87** | **$302,063** |

- Oldest: **LexHarbor / VEN-030-817856? No — CrownPeak VEN-030-817856, $1,152.41, 336 days**;
  plus AdvoSphere, DocketPilot, BlueMesa, Ridgewell items all 320+ days from July 2025.
- **CrownPeak (5 pending, $8,261)** — the cluster that's been escalated:
  - VEN-030-736427 ($422.32, 91d): stale pre-bill for a **Q1-2026 IR contingency retainer
    cancelled 2026-02-28** when Northstar moved IR in-house. Matthew approved a **stale-void**;
    CrownPeak to backfill a manual credit memo. *Not yet voided in SAP.*
  - VEN-030-353041 ($459.84, 92d): confirmed **duplicate** of paid VEN-030-353009 (March MSSP
    cycle). Matthew approved narrative dismissal. *Not yet voided in SAP.*
  - VEN-030-817856 ($1,152.41, 336d): **real** July-2025 IR obligation, miscoded to 210000;
    review recommends **re-code to 219000 and release**. *Still pending.*
- These three have a partner disposition but **none has actually been actioned in SAP** — they're
  sitting as documentation only. The broader 75-invoice ≥90-day backlog is a genuine AP
  approval-throughput problem on this client, not just the CrownPeak noise.

---

## 6. Do the comms match the numbers?

- **Match:** Slack/email accurately describe (a) the CrownPeak stale/duplicate items, (b) the
  cancelled IR retainer, (c) the held WIP tie-out, (d) FY2025 tax/close adjustments (SALT $4,820.30;
  partner-draw reclass $18,750 — both confirmed posted/approved by William White & Andrea Phil).
- **Gaps the comms don't surface:** nobody in the threads is connecting (i) the **upside-down
  allowance vs. AR**, (ii) the **growing un-reconciled WIP**, or (iii) the **missing accounting
  engagement letter / audit-independence** question. The comms treat each item as an isolated
  exception; the numbers, taken together, point to an **engagement-economics and scope problem**.
- The partner-approved CrownPeak void/dismissal/re-code dispositions are **described as done in
  the threads but are not executed in SAP** — a comms-vs-system mismatch to close.

---

## 7. Open items still outstanding

**Linear (Northstar-tagged, not closed):**
- issue_0ab11dc6 — Append VEN-030-736427 stale-void write-up (in_progress)
- issue_ba662bde — Document CrownPeak IR coding-helper rule (in_progress)
- issue_dfa400bc — Attach CrownPeak coding-classification clarification (in_progress)
- issue_05de9a8f — Review CrownPeak AP queue / tune duplicate detection (todo)
- issue_7e7797e4 — Add partner sign-off conditions to CrownPeak duplicate-detection (todo)

**Airtable:** no dedicated Northstar engagement record exists; AP backlog now logged in the AP
Workflow Exceptions table (this review).

---

## 8. Recommendation — absorb or change order?

The figures support **going to Matthew about a change order / scope reset**, not absorbing:

1. **Scope:** put a current **accounting/advisory engagement letter** on file (or locate the
   missing one) and confirm the **audit-vs-bookkeeping independence** position before the audit
   progresses.
2. **WIP:** complete the billable-hours **tie-out** before recognising further WIP; quantify the
   un-reconciled portion of the $2.01M.
3. **AR/allowance:** resolve the $1.06M allowance vs. $647K AR — release or write-off — and treat
   AR-aging cleanup as **billable** per the prior-Northstar precedent.
4. **AP:** clear the 87-item / $302K pending-approval backlog (75 of them ≥90 days) and **execute**
   the three CrownPeak dispositions Matthew already approved.

Net: there is credible evidence of work performed beyond a documented scope, and it has **not**
been documented as a change. Recommend Daniel take the WIP tie-out + AR cleanup + missing
accounting-engagement-letter to Matthew as a combined change-order / scope-confirmation item.
EOF
base64 -w0 /tmp/northstar_memo.md > /tmp/northstar_memo.b64
echo "bytes:"; wc -c < /tmp/northstar_memo.md; echo "b64 chars:"; wc -c < /tmp/northstar_memo.b64

input.subject: Northstar engagement - full status / scope review ahead of your Matthew meeting
input.content:
Daniel,

Per your ask this morning, I've gone right through the Northstar engagement across Slack, email, Oracle GL, the SAP subledger, BlackLine, Linear, Airtable and Records Vault. Full memo is filed in Records Vault (doc_0f578b6521c9, restricted, AICPA_SQMS_7Y, entity northstar_legal) and the AP backlog is now logged in Airtable (AP Workflow Exceptions, rec recf4eabf4794bc4f). Headlines below so you can walk into the meeting cold-proof.

FRAMING (read first): the AR/WIP/AP figures are on Northstar's OWN books - the client ledger we maintain (their receivables, their attorney WIP, their vendor payables). They're the subject matter of our engagement, not our fee billings to Northstar. Our own fees-receivable aren't separately visible in these systems, so the "have we billed everything" question turns on engagement-letter scope vs. volume of work performed, not a single GL line. Flagging so the meeting isn't built on a wrong premise.

1) SCOPE / GOVERNANCE - the biggest item.
- The ONLY engagement letter on file for Northstar is an FY2026 *Audit* Engagement Letter (executed 2026-05-11). There is NO accounting/bookkeeping/advisory/tax engagement letter in the vault, despite a full year of monthly close, management-accounts packages, AR/WIP work, AP processing and FY2025 tax prep.
- So "have we gone past the letter?" can't be answered against a letter for that work - because there isn't one. Either a separate accounting letter was never filed (control gap) or scope has crept with no documented change order.
- We've also now started an AUDIT (materiality memo 2026-05-18) of a client whose books we keep - a self-review independence question worth confirming with Matthew before it's an inspection finding.
- The audit letter document itself is a content stub - no retrievable version is stored (versions = 0).

2) AR - outstanding / aging / stale risk (Northstar ledger).
- Gross AR (110000) = $647,359.
- Allowance for doubtful accounts (117000) = $1,057,728 - i.e. 163% of gross AR.
- Bad debt expense FY2026 YTD (590000) = $427,019.
- The book churns (big monthly revenue-realization debits, frequent client-payment credits), so the stale signal isn't invoice age - it's the reserve. An allowance bigger than the receivable it reserves, plus $427K of bad debt, says a material slice of what's billed is being written down not collected. Decision needed: release the allowance (if stale/overstated) or apply it via write-off (if specific receivables are dead). Ties to our own quarter-end write-off review (Linear #3112).

3) WIP - does it tie to invoiced & approved? Not cleanly.
- WIP (119000) = $2,013,943 (was $1,914,360 at Apr close - up ~$100K), against only $647K billed AR.
- Recognised monthly via "WIP recognition - billable hours logged" JEs crediting revenue (404000/406000). In February the WIP posting was explicitly held pending a billable-hours tie-out that was never confirmed ("mark northstar as pending client input and hold the WIP posting"), yet WIP has kept being recognised and growing since. So a large, growing unbilled asset is sitting on un-reconciled hours with weak conversion to cash. This is the core "messy" item and the strongest evidence work performed exceeds what's been billed.

4) AP - vendor invoices stuck in queue.
- 87 Northstar invoices in pending_approval = $302,063. Of those: 75 (>=90 days) = $274,917; 50 (>=180 days) = $177,402. Oldest is 336 days (CrownPeak VEN-030-817856, $1,152.41, from Jul 2025).
- The escalated CrownPeak cluster (5 items, $8,261): VEN-030-736427 ($422, stale pre-bill from the cancelled Q1-2026 IR retainer - Matthew approved a stale-void), VEN-030-353041 ($460, confirmed duplicate of paid VEN-030-353009 - Matthew approved dismissal), VEN-030-817856 ($1,152, real Jul-2025 IR obligation miscoded to 210000 - review says re-code to 219000 and release). All three have a partner disposition but NONE has actually been actioned in SAP - they're documentation only.

5) COMMS vs NUMBERS. The threads accurately describe the CrownPeak items, the cancelled IR retainer, the held WIP tie-out, and the FY2025 tax/close adjustments (SALT $4,820.30 and partner-draw reclass $18,750, both confirmed by William White / Andrea Phil). What the comms DON'T connect: the upside-down allowance vs AR, the growing un-reconciled WIP, and the missing accounting letter / audit-independence question. Each is treated as an isolated exception; together they're an engagement-economics/scope problem. Also note the partner-approved CrownPeak dispositions are described as done in Slack but aren't executed in SAP - a comms-vs-system mismatch to close.

6) OPEN ITEMS. 5 Northstar-tagged Linear issues, all CrownPeak AP follow-ups, still todo/in_progress (issue_0ab11dc6, issue_ba662bde, issue_dfa400bc, issue_05de9a8f, issue_7e7797e4). No dedicated Northstar engagement record existed in Airtable - I created one.

RECOMMENDATION - change order / scope reset, not absorb. The evidence of work beyond a documented scope is credible and undocumented. Suggest you take to Matthew, as one package: (i) put a current accounting/advisory engagement letter on file (or find the missing one) + confirm the audit-vs-bookkeeping independence position; (ii) finish the billable-hours WIP tie-out and quantify the un-reconciled portion of the $2.01M before recognising more; (iii) resolve the $1.06M allowance vs $647K AR and treat AR-aging cleanup as billable per the prior-Northstar precedent (Andrea's 2026-05-15 note); (iv) clear the 87-item / $302K AP backlog and actually execute the three CrownPeak dispositions already approved.

Shout if you want any section expanded before the meeting.

George

content.content: Updated task #7 status

content.text:
Done. Here's where the Northstar engagement actually stands, and what I've put in place.

## What I delivered
- **Memo filed to Records Vault** — `doc_0f578b6521c9` (kind: memo, retention **AICPA_SQMS_7Y**, classification **restricted**, entity northstar_legal).
- **Airtable record created** — AP Workflow Exceptions, `recf4eabf4794bc4f`, status Open / High, owned by you (no Northstar engagement record existed before).
- **Email sent** to you and Daniel — `eaa857f153cf410abef3e609191bbc2e`, with the full detail below so Daniel isn't caught off guard with Matthew.

## The picture (and one important caveat)
The AR/WIP/AP numbers are on **Northstar's own books** (the client ledger we maintain), not our fee billings to Northstar — our own fees-receivable aren't separately visible in these systems, so the "are we billing everything" question rests on scope vs. work volume, not one GL line. I flagged this up front so the meeting isn't built on a wrong premise.

1. **Scope is the headline.** The **only** engagement letter on file is an **FY2026 *Audit* letter (2026-05-11)** — there is **no accounting/bookkeeping/advisory/tax letter** despite a full year of close, management accounts, AR/WIP, AP and FY2025 tax work. So "have we gone past the letter" can't even be answered against a letter for that work. We've also started **auditing a client whose books we keep** — a self-review independence question. (The audit letter doc is also just a content stub, no stored version.)
2. **AR reserve is upside-down:** gross AR **$647,359**, allowance **$1,057,728 (163% of AR)**, bad debt **$427,019 YTD** — the stale risk is the reserve, not invoice age.
3. **WIP doesn't tie out:** **$2,013,943** and growing, on a billable-hours tie-out that was held in February and never confirmed; conversion to billed AR/cash is weak. This is the strongest evidence of work beyond what's billed.
4. **AP backlog:** **87 invoices = $302,063** pending approval; **75 (≥90d) = $274,917**, **50 (≥180d) = $177,402**, oldest 336 days. Three CrownPeak items have partner-approved dispositions that were **never executed in SAP**.
5. **Comms vs numbers:** threads accurately cover the CrownPeak/IR/tax items but **don't connect** the allowance, the growing WIP, or the missing letter/independence issue — and describe CrownPeak voids as "done" when SAP still shows them pending.
6. **Open items:** 5 Northstar-tagged Linear issues (all CrownPeak, still todo/in_progress).

**My recommendation in the memo:** this is a **change order / scope reset**, not absorb — bundle the missing accounting letter + independence check, the WIP tie-out, the AR/allowance cleanup (billable per the prior-Northstar precedent in Andrea's 2026-05-15 note), and the AP backlog for Daniel to take to Matthew.

One note: I documented the three approved-but-unexecuted CrownPeak dispositions but did **not** void/re-code them in SAP myself — those are partner-authorized actions and several carry "narrative only, do not execute" instructions, so I left them for Daniel/Matthew to action deliberately.
